data_type_evidence: experiment_code.py:100 - Wikipedia domain uses np.random.randint(50, 200, seq_len) to generate synthetic token sequences
experiment_code.py:102 - Code domain uses np.random.randint(200, 300, seq_len // 4) with np.tile for synthetic repetitive patterns
experiment_code.py:107-108 - Scientific domain uses np.random.randint for two concatenated ranges (300-350, 350-400)
experiment_code.py:112 - Conversational domain uses np.random.randint(400, 450, seq_len)
experiment_code.py:88-95 - TinyStories and OpenWebText attempt load_dataset() but with bare except fallback to None (synthetic fallback at lines 123, 134)
experiment_code.py:116,127 - Even when real data loads, 'tokenization' is just ord(c) % vocab_size (character ordinal mod 500), not actual tokenization
experiment_code.py:73 - num_samples_per_domain=400, seq_len=32, vocab_size=500 — tiny synthetic-scale parameters
4 of 6 domains always generate purely random integer sequences; 2 domains have unreliable real-data loading with synthetic fallback
experiment_data.npy files exist confirming experiments were actually executed (not fabricated)
